void convertToBase(int num, int base, const string& digits, string& result) {
    if (num == 0) {
        result = "0";
        return;
    }
    while (num > 0) {
        int remainder = num % base;
        result = digits[remainder] + result; // prepend to get correct order
        num = num / base;
    }
}

string concatHex36(int n) {

class Solution {
public:
    string concatHex36(int n) {
        int n2 = n * n;
        string hex;
        while (n2 > 0) {

            n3 = n3 / 36;

        return hex + base36;
    }
};

Now, what about the time and space complexity?

Time complexity: For each conversion, the number of steps depends on the number of digits in the converted number.

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string concatHex36(int n) {
        int n2 = n * n;
        string hex;
        while (n2 > 0) {
            int rem = n2 % 16;
            hex = "0123456789ABCDEF"[rem] + hex;
            n2 = n2 / 16;
        }
        int n3 = n * n * n;
        string base36;
        while (n3 > 0) {
            int rem = n3 % 36;
            base36 = "0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"[rem] + base36;
            n3 = n3 / 36;
        }
        return hex + base36;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(log n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution converts n² to hexadecimal and n³ to base-36 by repeatedly dividing by the base and prepending digits. It uses direct string indexing for digit conversion, ensuring efficiency with logarithmic time complexity relative to the input size. The approach avoids helper functions for minimal overhead.
